name: Purification Domain
description: The Purification Domain believes sickness, mutation, and other forms of bodily corruption are signs of a corresponding spiritual malady. Virtue can't exist alongside impurity. The wickedness of humanity and other mortal, sentient creatures is a contagion that manifests plagues such as the Weeping Pox. When there's a risk of such corruption spreading, there's only one surefire way to contain the rot and atone for the sins that led to this point: Burn it out. Purge Sickness with Suffering.

features.name: Cleanse with Fire
features.description: When you deal damage with a cantrip or an attack with a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can deal an additional {@damage 1d8} Fire damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB} (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Purification Domain Spells
features.description: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Purification Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Unclean Brand
features.description: When you hit a creature with a melee attack with a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, instead of dealing the strike's normal damage, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to sear a symbol into the creature's flesh, marking it with a glowing brand for 1 minute. During that time, the creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws against your spells. Additionally, the creature gains {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Fire damage you deal, even if it normally has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} or {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Fire damage.

features.name: Ward Against Corruption
features.description: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end diseases and against any effect that would change your form, such as the {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} spell. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch a willing creature to grant this benefit, but the creature takes Fire damage equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1). This damage ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}. Once you grant this benefit, it lasts for 1 hour or until you grant this benefit again.

features.name: Sear Imperfections
features.description: You can cast {@spell Lesser Restoration|XPHB} and {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB} on a willing creature without expending spell slots and without Material components, but the target takes {@damage 1d6} Fire damage for each level of the spell slot immediately after you cast it. This damage ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}.
